(a) The public safety director shall enter negotiations with an appropriate person or entity of a state bordering this state for the purpose of applying the exemption contained in Section 522.004 (Applicability) (a)(1) to residents of that state.

(b) The public safety director may enter an agreement to apply the exemption contained in Section 522.004 (Applicability) (a)(1) to residents of a bordering state only if that state extends a similar exemption to residents of this state.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 522. Commercial Driver's Licenses
##### § 522.007 — Exemption for Neighboring States

(a) The public safety director shall enter negotiations with an appropriate person or entity of a state bordering this state for the purpose of applying the exemption contained in Section 522.004 (Applicability) (a)(1) to residents of that state.

(b) The public safety director may enter an agreement to apply the exemption contained in Section 522.004 (Applicability) (a)(1) to residents of a bordering state only if that state extends a similar exemption to residents of this state.

*Added by Acts 1997, 75th Leg., ch. 1061, Sec. 14, eff. Sept. 1, 1997.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: The public safety director shall enter negotiations with an appropriate person or entity of a state bordering this state for the purpose of applying the exemption contained in Section 522.004 (Applicability) (a)(1) to residents of that state.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The public safety director may enter an agreement to apply the exemption contained in Section 522.004 (Applicability) (a)(1) to residents of a bordering state only if that state extends a similar exemption to residents of this state.

history.raw: Added by Acts 1997, 75th Leg., ch. 1061, Sec. 14, eff. Sept. 1, 1997.
